# News Summaries Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Extend `GET /news` to include a `summary` markdown field per article, generated by Groq from content extracted via Jina Reader API, with graceful fallback to NewsAPI title+description.

**Architecture:** New `jinaService.ts` handles content extraction with fallback logic. `groqService.ts` gets a new `summarizeArticle()` function. `newsService.ts` gets source blacklisting. `newsController.ts` orchestrates the two new steps after article selection. The `NewsArticle` interface gains a `summary` field and a `description` field (needed for fallback). Cache stores the enriched articles.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, axios (already installed), Jina Reader API (free, no key), Groq API (existing), Express

---

### Task 1: Add `description` to NewsArticle and source blacklist to newsService

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/services/newsService.ts`

The `description` field from NewsAPI is needed as fallback content for Jina failures. The source blacklist filters out known bad sources before LLM selection.

**Step 1: Update `NewsArticle` interface — add `description` field**

In `src/services/newsService.ts`, change the interface from:
```typescript
export interface NewsArticle {
  title: string;
  url: string;
  source: string;
  publishedAt: string;
  category: 'global' | 'argentina' | 'geopolitics' | 'watchlist';
}
```
To:
```typescript
export interface NewsArticle {
  title: string;
  url: string;
  source: string;
  publishedAt: string;
  category: 'global' | 'argentina' | 'geopolitics' | 'watchlist';
  description: string;
}
```

**Step 2: Add source blacklist constant after the `API_KEY` line**

```typescript
const SOURCE_BLACKLIST = ['Yahoo Entertainment'];
```

**Step 3: Map `description` in `fetchCategory`**

Change the `.map()` in `fetchCategory` from:
```typescript
  return (response.data.articles ?? []).map((a: any) => ({
    title: a.title,
    url: a.url,
    source: a.source?.name ?? '',
    publishedAt: a.publishedAt,
    category,
  }));
```
To:
```typescript
  return (response.data.articles ?? []).map((a: any) => ({
    title: a.title,
    url: a.url,
    source: a.source?.name ?? '',
    publishedAt: a.publishedAt,
    description: a.description ?? '',
    category,
  }));
```

**Step 4: Apply source blacklist at end of `fetchAllNews`**

Change the final filter in `fetchAllNews` from:
```typescript
  return all.filter(article => {
    if (!article.title || !article.url || seen.has(article.url)) return false;
    seen.add(article.url);
    return true;
  });
```
To:
```typescript
  return all.filter(article => {
    if (!article.title || !article.url || seen.has(article.url)) return false;
    if (SOURCE_BLACKLIST.includes(article.source)) return false;
    seen.add(article.url);
    return true;
  });
```

**Step 5: Smoke-test**

Start the server (`npm run dev`) and hit `GET /news` — confirm it still works and Yahoo Entertainment articles are absent. Then stop the server.

**Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add src/services/newsService.ts
git commit -m "feat: add description field to NewsArticle and source blacklist"
```

---

### Task 2: Create JinaService

**Files:**
- Create: `src/services/jinaService.ts`

**Step 1: Write the service**

```typescript
import axios from 'axios';
import { NewsArticle } from './newsService';

const JINA_BASE = 'https://r.jina.ai/';
const JINA_TIMEOUT_MS = 8000;
const MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH = 200;

const JINA_DOMAIN_BLACKLIST = ['apnews.com'];

function getDomain(url: string): string {
  try {
    return new URL(url).hostname.replace('www.', '');
  } catch {
    return '';
  }
}

function isBlocked(content: string): boolean {
  return content.toLowerCase().includes('blocked') || content.length < MIN_CONTENT_LENGTH;
}

export async function fetchArticleContent(article: NewsArticle): Promise<string> {
  const domain = getDomain(article.url);

  if (JINA_DOMAIN_BLACKLIST.includes(domain)) {
    console.log(`[jina] domain blacklisted, using fallback: ${domain}`);
    return `${article.title}. ${article.description}`;
  }

  try {
    const response = await axios.get(`${JINA_BASE}${article.url}`, {
      timeout: JINA_TIMEOUT_MS,
      headers: { Accept: 'text/plain' },
    });
    const content: string = response.data ?? '';

    if (isBlocked(content)) {
      console.log(`[jina] blocked or too short, using fallback: ${article.url}`);
      return `${article.title}. ${article.description}`;
    }

    // Truncate to ~3000 chars to keep Groq prompt manageable
    return content.slice(0, 3000);
  } catch (err) {
    console.log(`[jina] fetch failed, using fallback: ${article.url}`);
    return `${article.title}. ${article.description}`;
  }
}
```

**Step 2: Smoke-test manually**

Temporarily add to `src/index.ts` startup:
```typescript
import { fetchArticleContent } from './services/jinaService';
const testArticle = {
  title: 'Test',
  url: 'https://www.infobae.com',
  source: 'Infobae',
  publishedAt: '',
  description: 'fallback description',
  category: 'argentina' as const,
};
fetchArticleContent(testArticle).then(c => console.log('Jina content length:', c.length));
```
Run `npm run dev`, confirm you see a content length > 200. Remove the temp code. Stop the server.

**Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/services/jinaService.ts
git commit -m "feat: add JinaService for article content extraction with fallback"
```

---

### Task 3: Add `summarizeArticle` to GroqService

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/services/groqService.ts`

**Step 1: Add the function at the end of the file**

```typescript
export async function summarizeArticle(article: NewsArticle, content: string): Promise<string> {
  const prompt = `Dado el siguiente contenido de un artículo periodístico, escribí un resumen conciso en español en formato markdown (máximo 150 palabras). Usá un título H3 y 2-3 párrafos breves.\n\nTítulo: ${article.title}\nContenido: ${content}`;

  const response = await axios.post(
    GROQ_URL,
    {
      model: process.env.GROQ_MODEL ?? 'llama-3.3-70b-versatile',
      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }],
      temperature: 0.3,
    },
    {
      headers: {
        Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.GROQ_API_KEY}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
    }
  );

  return response.data.choices[0].message.content.trim();
}
```

Note: `GROQ_URL` and `axios` are already imported/defined at the top of the file. `NewsArticle` needs to be imported — add it to the existing import:
```typescript
import { NewsArticle } from './newsService';
```

**Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add src/services/groqService.ts
git commit -m "feat: add summarizeArticle to GroqService"
```

---

### Task 4: Update NewsArticle type and wire everything in NewsController

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/controllers/newsController.ts`

The controller needs to:
1. Fetch content for each of the 5 articles in parallel via Jina
2. Summarize each in parallel via Groq
3. Attach the `summary` field to each article before caching

**Step 1: Update imports**

Change the imports at the top of `newsController.ts` to:
```typescript
import { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { fetchAllNews } from '../services/newsService';
import { selectTopArticles, summarizeArticle } from '../services/groqService';
import { fetchArticleContent } from '../services/jinaService';
import { NewsArticle } from '../services/newsService';
```

**Step 2: Update cache type to include summary**

The `NewsArticle` interface now has `description` but not `summary`. `summary` is added after enrichment. Define a local enriched type:

Change the cache declaration from:
```typescript
let cache: { articles: NewsArticle[]; cachedAt: number } | null = null;
```
To:
```typescript
type EnrichedArticle = NewsArticle & { summary: string };

let cache: { articles: EnrichedArticle[]; cachedAt: number } | null = null;
```

**Step 3: Update `getNews` to fetch content and summarize**

Replace the pipeline inside the try block (after the cache check) from:
```typescript
      const articles = await fetchAllNews();
      if (articles.length === 0) throw new Error('No se pudieron obtener artículos de NewsAPI');
      const top = await selectTopArticles(articles);

      cache = { articles: top, cachedAt: Date.now() };
      res.status(200).json(top);
```
To:
```typescript
      const articles = await fetchAllNews();
      if (articles.length === 0) throw new Error('No se pudieron obtener artículos de NewsAPI');
      const top = await selectTopArticles(articles);

      const enriched: EnrichedArticle[] = await Promise.all(
        top.map(async (article) => {
          const content = await fetchArticleContent(article);
          const summary = await summarizeArticle(article, content);
          return { ...article, summary };
        })
      );

      cache = { articles: enriched, cachedAt: Date.now() };
      res.status(200).json(enriched);
```

**Step 4: Smoke-test end-to-end**

Run `npm run dev`, wait for startup, then:
```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/resetnews
curl http://localhost:3000/news
```

Expected: JSON array of 5 articles, each with a `summary` field containing markdown (H3 heading + paragraphs). The request will take ~15-30 seconds on first call (Jina + Groq × 5).

Second call should return instantly from cache.

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/controllers/newsController.ts
git commit -m "feat: enrich /news articles with Jina content extraction and Groq summaries"
```
